## [00:00 - 02:45]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Ding ding ding. Let's go. So I've talked before about the dangers of optimizing for traffic or engagement. Because Facebook doesn't know what quality traffic, for example, is. But that doesn't mean you shouldn't ever try to drive traffic. That's a big part of my strategy. So what do I do? Do I just throw my money away? No, the goal, what you have to do is be able to tell Facebook what quality traffic looks like. So that they can optimize for it. Okay? So the way that I do that, I have created custom pixel events. On my site, they fire if you've read a blog post for at least 60 seconds. Or if you've scrolled through a page at least 75%. And then I with those custom events, I can then create a conversions objective campaign. Optimizing for conversions. Optimizing for a specific type of conversion. That say someone spent 60 seconds or more on my website. So now Facebook knows in my results column, if all someone did was click on a link, go to my site and abandon. That's not a result. That's not what I'm looking for. Another person goes clicks, reads blog posts, spends 60 seconds on there. Ding ding ding. That's a result. We're happy. I know you're happy, John.
